titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Bea Nettles, Photographic Portraits for Mountain Dream Tarot
Nettles (Bea), Photographic Portraits for Mountain Dream Tarot
titlestmt.author: Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 2, 2024 at 03:02

did.unittitle: Bea Nettles, Photographic Portraits for Mountain Dream Tarot

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Composite photographic prints with hand painting created between 1970 and 1975 by Bea Nettles as images for Mountain Dream Tarot , published approximately 1975 and distributed by the Light Impressions Corporation. The collection also includes three photographic prints of her children and a student created in 2000. The composite photographic portraits depict the subjects as tarot cards based on the Rider-Waite Tarot published by the Rider Company in 1909 based on the instructions of academic and mystic Arthur Edward Waite (1857-1942) and illustrated by Pamela Colman Smith (1878-1951). The portraits document artists and students at the Penland School of Craft in Penland, North Carolina, created primarily during residencies by Nettles, as well as portraits of friends created in Rochester, New York, and portraits of family members likely created in Gainesville, Florida, and Rochester, New York, as well as self-portraits of Nettles.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Bea Nettles on the Mary Flagler Cary Fund, 2013.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Major Arcana, 1970-1975. II. Minor Arcana - Cups, 1970-2001. III. Minor Arcana - Pentacles, 1970-1975. IV. Minor Arcana - Swords, 1970-2001. V. Minor Arcana - Wands, 1970-2001. VI. Box Cover Art, 1970-1975.

bioghist.head: Bea Nettles (born 1946)
bioghist.p: Bea Nettles is a fine art photographer and author. She studied painting and printmaking as an undergraduate at the University of Florida and earned a master's degree in painting from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign in 1970. Nettles taught photography and artists' books from 1970-2007 at Rochester Institute of Technology, Tyler School of Art, and the University of Illinois. She creates semi-autobiographical works with themes related family relationships woven together with mythology and natural history, and often in dream-like juxtapositions.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Bea Nettles, Photographic Portraits for Mountain Dream Tarot . General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Descriptions of subjects derive from identifications provided by Nettles about the subject and location of the image as well as the residence of the subject in 1970 and 2013 when applicable. Single year dates for prints derive from a memoir by Nettles collected for the Jane Kessler Memorial Archives at the Penland School of Craft in Penland, North Carolina.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composite photographic prints with hand painting created between 1970 and 1975 by Bea Nettles as images for Mountain Dream Tarot , published approximately 1975 and distributed by the Light Impressions Corporation. The collection also includes three photographic prints of her children and a student created in 2001. The composite photographic portraits depict the subjects as tarot cards based on the Rider-Waite Tarot published by the Rider Company in 1909 based on the instructions of academic and mystic Arthur Edward Waite (1857-1942) and illustrated by Pamela Colman Smith (1878-1951). The portraits document artists and students at the Penland School of Craft in Penland, North Carolina, created primarily during residencies by Nettles, as well as portraits of friends created in Rochester, New York, and portraits of family members likely created in Gainesville, Florida, and Rochester, New York, as well as self-portraits of Nettles.

Portraits of artists and students at the Penland School of Craft in Penland, North Carolina, 1970-1975, include Garret Bomen, Jerry Brown, Bill Brown, Sr., Bill Brown, Jr., David M. Dale, Peter Desmond, Don Drumm, Robert Forsythe, John B. Garcia, Ronald Lee Garfinkle, Melissa Fay Johnson Garfinkle, Thomas Gentille, John Parker Glick, Gary Swaffield Griffin, Patricia Janel Melott Griffin, Keith Allen Hatcher, Mary Flo Hecko Hatcher, Roland K. Jahn, True Kelly, David W. Herman, Mary Law, Austin Lowrey, John McDail, Catherine Morony, Jane Goslin Peiser, Mark Peiser, Ron Propst, Tamara Propst, Geraldine Scalone, Randall Ashton Scherr, Peter Spider, Robert Townsend, Albert Sinclair Vrana, Marie Johanna Van Rennes Vrana, Susan Louise Vrana-Brogan, and Sue Williams. There is also a portrait of Jason Weidemier in 2001.
Portraits of friends at Rochester, New York, 1971-1975, include Stephanie Smart Button, Kimberly Smart Dudley, Steve Kahn, Thomas Kekic, David K. Smart, and Nancy Ann Wise Smart.

Portraits of Bea Nettles' family created in Gainesville, Florida, and Rochester, New York, 1970-1975, include her spouse Lionel Suntop, her parents Grace Noble Nettles and Victor Fleetwood Nettles, Sr., her sister Elizabeth Grace Nettles Clark and spouse John Eastham Clark, her sister Constance Louise Nettles Scott, and her brothers John Robert Nettles and Victor Fleetwood Nettles, Jr. Portraits of Bea Nettles' children created in 2001 include Rachel Starr Suntop and Gavin Lazar Suntop.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Bea Nettles, Photographic Portraits for Mountain Dream Tarot , is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composite photographic portraits with hand painting by Bea Nettles that depict the subjects as Major Arcana based on the Rider-Waite Tarot published by the Rider Company in 1909 based on the instructions of academic and mystic Arthur Edward Waite (1857-1942) and illustrated by Pamela Colman Smith (1878-1951).

The Major Arcana are numbered and named cards in a divination tarot pack, and the name originally given by occultists to the trump cards of a normal tarot pack used for playing card games typically numbered from 0 to 21.

The subjects of the portraits document artists at the Penland School of Craft in Penland, North Carolina, created primarily during residencies by Nettles, as well as portraits of friends created in Rochester, New York, and portraits of family members likely created in Gainesville, Florida, and Rochester, New York.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged sequentially by the leading number associated with each card

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: David W. Herman was a student at the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of David W. Herman from North Bennington, Vermont; photographed at the Loom House at the Penland School of Craft, Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Thomas Gentille (born 1936) is a jeweler and educator as well as a metals instructor at the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Thomas Gentille from New York City; photographed in front of Horner Hall at the Penland School of Craft, Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: True Kelly (born 1936) is an art teacher, a photographer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of True Kelly from Penland, North Carolina; photographed at Horner Hall at the Penland School of Craft, Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Grace Noble Nettles (1919-2007) was a poet and an English instructor at Santa Fe College, Gainesville, Florida, as well as mother of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Grace Noble Nettles from Gainesville, Florida

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Victor Fleetwood Nettles (1914-2002) was a professor of horticulture at the University of Florida and the father of Bea Nettles. He also served as a lieutenant colonel in the United States Army during World War II and awarded the Bronze Star.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Victor Fleetwood Nettles from Gainesville, Florida

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Keith Allen Hatcher (1934-2017) was a printmaker, watercolor painter and basket maker as professor of art at Southern Connecticut State University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Keith Allen Hatcher from New Haven, Connecticut; photographed at Horner Hall at the Penland School of Craft, Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Ron Propst (born 1944) is a studio potter and Tamara Propst (born 1946) is a weaver. They established The Other Half gallery in Winston-Salem, North Carolina, in 1983.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Ron Propst and Tamara Propst from Penland, North Carolina; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Thomas Kekic (1943-1986) was a glass artist as well as the owner and operator of Bristol Glassworks in Canandaigua, New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Tom Kekic from Rochester, New York; photographed at Penland, North Carolina; affixed photographic print on recto of sculptures, 6 x 6.8 cm

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Catherine Morony was a resident artist at the Penland School of Craft in Penland, North Carolina, and specialized in natural textile dyes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Catherine Morony from Penland, North Carolina; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Roland K. Jahn (1934-2023) was a German American studio glass artist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Roland K. Jahn from Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Robert Nettles (born 1948) is a lawyer and brother of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of John Robert Nettles from Gainesville, Florida; image includes portraits of a bull, eagle, and lion

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mary Flo Hecko Hatcher (born 1937), known as Flo Hatcher, is a print maker and widow of Keith Allen Hatcher (1934-2017).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Mary Flo Hecko Hatcher from New Haven, Connecticut; photographed at Horner Hall at the Penland School of Craft, Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Robert Nettles (born 1948) is a lawyer and brother of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of John Robert Nettles from Gainesville, Florida

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Street portrait of a dancer in Mexico; subject may be Peruvian performing the danza de las tijeras, or scissors dance, is a traditional dance from the Andes.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Elizabeth Grace Nettles Clark (born 1953) is a sister of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Elizabeth Grace Nettles Clark from Gainesville, Florida

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Roland K. Jahn (1934-2023) was a German American studio glass artist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Roland K. Jahn, From Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Eastham Clark (born 1952) is a medical doctor specializing in sports medicine married to Elizabeth Grace Nettle Clark, and a brother-in-law of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of John Eastham Clark from Gainesville, Florida

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Sue Williams was a ceramics student at the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Sue Williams from Florida; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Geraldine Scalone (born 1941) taught weaving the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Geraldine Scalone from Mystic, Connecticut; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mary Law was a ceramics student at the Penland School of Craft. Portia Propst Bodenheimer (born approximately 1968) is a daughter of Ron Propst (born 1944) and Tamara Propst (born 1946).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Mary Law from Berkeley, California as the sun and Portia Propst riding a horse; Law photographed outside the clay pottery studio at the Penland School of Craft, Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Elizabeth Grace Nettles Clark (born 1953) is a sister of Bea Nettles. Eric Norman Shurtleff (born 1954) is a son of Byron Parker Shurtleff (1929-1999).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Elizabeth Grace Nettles Clark from Gainesville, Florida, as the angel, and Eric Norman Shurtleff is the spirit photographed at the cemetery at Penland, North Carolina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Self-portrait of Bea Nettles; likely photographed at Penland, North Carolina; photograph affixed to recto replicates the imagery for Wheel , which includes a portrait of John Robert Nettles as well as a bull, eagle, and lion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composite photographic portraits with hand painting by Bea Nettles created 1970-2001, bulk 1970-1975, that depict the subjects as Major Arcana based on the Rider-Waite Tarot published by the Rider Company in 1909 based on the instructions of academic and mystic Arthur Edward Waite (1857-1942) and illustrated by Pamela Colman Smith (1878-1951).

The Minor Arcana are suit cards in a divination tarot pack. Each suit consists of four court cards (face cards) and ten pip cards numbered one (ace) to ten. The cups correspond to the hearts of modern playing cards.

The subjects of the portraits document artists at the Penland School of Craft in Penland, North Carolina, created primarily during residencies by Nettles, as well as portraits of friends created in Rochester, New York, and portraits of family members likely created in Gainesville, Florida, and Rochester, New York.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in ascending order of face value.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert Forsythe was a photographer who accompanied Evon Streetman (born 1932) to the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hand of Robert Forsythe from Tallahassee, Florida; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Eastham Clark (born 1952) is a medical doctor specializing in sports medicine married to Elizabeth Grace Nettle Clark (born 1953), and a brother-in-law of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of John Eastham Clark and Elizabeth Grace Nettle Clark from Gainesville, Florida; family members of Bea Nettles

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Constance Louise Nettles Scott (born 1956) and Elizabeth Grace Nettles Clark (born 1953) are sisters of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Bea Nettles with Constance Louise Nettles Scott and Elizabeth Grace Nettles Clark

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: William "Bill" Joseph Brown (born 1955) is owner of the Anvil Arts Studio and the eldest son of Jane Comfort Brennan Brown (born 1931), an arts administrator, and William "Bill" Joseph Brown (1923-1992), a designer and director of the Penland School of Crafts from 1962 to 1983.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of William Joseph Brown, Jr., from Penland, North Carolina; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert Townsend was a glasswork student the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Robert Townsend from Saint Paul, Minnesota; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Kimberly Smart Dudley (born 1968) and Stephanie Smart Button (born 1970) are daughters of Nancy Ann Wise Smart (born 1942) and David K. Smart (1939-2016), who operated a vacuum cleaning business in Ionia, New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Kim and Stephanie Smart from Ionia, New York; photographed at Rochester, New York

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Victor Fleetwood Nettles (born 1946) is a Doctor of Veterinary Medicine and former director of the Southeastern Cooperative Wildlife Disease Study at the University of Georgia as well as a brother of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Victor Fleetwood Nettles from Athens, Georgia

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Ron Propst (born 1944) is a studio potter. With his wife Tamara Propst (born 1946), they established The Other Half gallery in Winston-Salem, North Carolina, in 1983.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Ron Propst from Penland, North Carolina; photographed near his pottery studio at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Don Drumm (born 1935) is a sculptor specializing in cast aluminum.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Don Drumm from Akron, Ohio; photographed holding one of his cast aluminum cups with others on a ledge behind him at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Victor Fleetwood Nettles (born 1946) is a Doctor of Veterinary Medicine and former director of the Southeastern Cooperative Wildlife Disease Study at the University of Georgia as well as a brother of Bea Nettles and widow of Margaret Eileen Minear (1946-2011).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Victor Fleetwood Nettles and Margaret Eileen Minear Nettles from Athens, Georgia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of an unidentified student in metal working at the Penland School of Craft; photographed at the steps of Horner Hall at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Rachel Starr Suntop (born 1978) is a textile artist and daughter of Bea Nettles and Lionel Suntop (born 1942).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Rachel Suntop from Urbana, Illinois; family member of Bea Nettles, photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: David M. Dale was likely a metalworking student at the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of David M. Dale from Santa Barbara, California; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Patricia Janel Melott Griffin (born 1948) is a teacher and spouse of Gary Griffin (born 1945), a metalsmith and educator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Patricia Janel Melott Griffin from Rochester, New York; photographed at Rochester, New York

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Gary Swaffield Griffin (born 1945) is a metalsmith and educator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Gary Swaffield Griffin from Rochester, New York; photographed at Rochester, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composite photographic portraits with hand painting by Bea Nettles that depict the subjects as Major Arcana based on the Rider-Waite Tarot published by the Rider Company in 1909 based on the instructions of academic and mystic Arthur Edward Waite (1857-1942) and illustrated by Pamela Colman Smith (1878-1951).

The Minor Arcana are suit cards in a divination tarot pack. Each suit consists of four court cards (face cards) and ten pip cards numbered one (ace) to ten. The pentacles correspond to the diamonds of modern playing cards.

The subjects of the portraits document artists at the Penland School of Craft in Penland, North Carolina, created primarily during residencies by Nettles, as well as portraits of friends created in Rochester, New York, and portraits of family members likely created in Gainesville, Florida, and Rochester, New York.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in ascending order of face value.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert Forsythe was a photographer who accompanied Evon Streetman (born 1932) to the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hand of Robert Forsythe from Tallahassee, Florida; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jerry Brown is multi-instrumentalist, producer, recording and mastering engineer as well as a co-owner of the Rubber Room Recording Studio in Chapel Hill, North Carolina. He is also the youngest son of Jane Comfort Brennan Brown (born 1931), an arts administrator, and William "Bill" Joseph Brown (1923-1992), a designer and director of the Penland School of Crafts from 1962 to 1983.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Jerry Brown from Penland, North Carolina; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Parker Glick (1938-2017) was a ceramicist and owner of Plum Tree Pottery in Farmington Hills, Michigan, as well as an instructor at the Penland School of Craft. Garret Bomen and John McDail were students at the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of three men identified from left to right as Garret Bomen, Jim McDail and John Parker Glick; photographed with a clay kiln at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Austin Lowrey is a graphic designer and professor of art at North Carolina University as well as a printmaking instructor at the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Austin Lowrey from Athens, Georgia; photographed at Horner Hall at the Penland School of Craft, Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Nancy Ann Wise Smart (born 1942) and David K. Smart (1939-2016) operated a vacuum cleaning business in Ionia, New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Nancy Ann Wise Smart and David K. Smart from Ionia, New York; photographed at Rochester, New York

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Lionel Suntop (born 1942) is a photographer and a founder of Light Impressions publishing as well as the spouse of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Lionel Suntop from Rochester, New York, with an unidentified person; likely photographed in Rochester, New York

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John B. Garcia was likely the son of an instructor at the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of John B. Garcia from Kenilworth, Illinois; photographed behind Horner Hall at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Don Drumm (born 1935) is a sculptor specializing in cast aluminum.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Don Drumm from Akron, Ohio; photographed in a shed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Susan Louise Vrana-Brogan (born 1947) was a student at the Penland School of Craft and an interior design contractor as well as a daughter of Marie Johanna Van Rennes Vrana (1926-2003) and Albert Sinclair Vrana (1921-1994).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Sue Vrana from New York City; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Ronald Lee Garfinkle (born 1943) is a potter and founder of Monroe Salt Works. Melissa Fay Johnson Garfinkle (born 1945) is a weaver and was married to Ron Garfinkle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Ronald Lee Garfinkle and Melissa Fay Johnson Garfinkle with their unidentified child from Penland, North Carolina; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Eastham Clark (born 1952) is a medical doctor specializing in sports medicine married to Elizabeth Grace Nettle Clark, and a brother-in-law of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of John Eastham Clark from Gainesville, Florida

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert Townsend was a glasswork student the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Robert Townsend from Saint Paul, Minnesota; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Self-portrait of Bea Nettles; photographed at Horner Hall at the Penland School of Craft, Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Lionel Suntop (born 1942) is a photographer and a founder of Light Impressions publishing as well as the spouse of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Lionel Suntop from Rochester, New York; likely photographed in Rochester, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composite photographic portraits with hand painting by Bea Nettles created 1970-2001, bulk 1970-1975, that depict the subjects as Major Arcana based on the Rider-Waite Tarot published by the Rider Company in 1909 based on the instructions of academic and mystic Arthur Edward Waite (1857-1942) and illustrated by Pamela Colman Smith (1878-1951).

The Minor Arcana are suit cards in a divination tarot pack. Each suit consists of four court cards (face cards) and ten pip cards numbered one (ace) to ten. The swords correspond to the spades of modern playing cards.

The subjects of the portraits document artists at the Penland School of Craft in Penland, North Carolina, created primarily during residencies by Nettles, as well as portraits of friends created in Rochester, New York, and portraits of family members likely created in Gainesville, Florida, and Rochester, New York.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in ascending order of face value.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph of a hand holding a sword aloft

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Constance Louise Nettles Scott (born 1956) is a sister of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Constance Louise Nettles Scott from Gainesville, Florida

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Still life photograph of three swords piercing a heart

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of an unidentified student in metal working at the Penland School of Craft; photographed at Horner Hall at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Constance Louise Nettles Scott (born 1956) is a sister of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Constance Louise Nettles Scott from Gainesville, Florida

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Constance Louise Nettles Scott (born 1956) is a sister of Bea Nettles. Elizabeth Grace Nettles Clark (born 1953) is a sister of Bea Nettles, and married to John Eastham Clark (born 1952) is a medical doctor specializing in sports medicine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Constance Louise Nettles Scott, Elizabeth Grace Nettles Clark, and John Eastham Clark from Gainesville, Florida

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Constance Louise Nettles Scott (born 1956) is a sister of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Constance Louise Nettles Scott from Gainesville, Florida

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Self-portrait of Bea Nettles; photographed at Horner Hall at the Penland School of Craft, Penland, North Carolina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Self-portrait of Bea Nettles; photographed at Horner Hall at the Penland School of Craft, Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Roland K. Jahn (1934-2023) was a German American studio glass artist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Roland K. Jahn from Philadelphia, Pennsylvania; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of an unidentified student in metal working student at the Penland School of Craft; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Gavin Lazar Suntop (born 1981) is a software engineer and son of Bea Nettles and Lionel Suntop (born 1942).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Gavin Lazar Suntop photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Randall Ashton Scherr (born 1949) was a son of Mary Ann Scherr (1921-2016), a jeweler and metals instructor at the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Randy Scherr from Ohio; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jane Goslin Peiser (1932-2022) was a clay artist and wife of Mark Peiser (born 1932).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Jane Goslin Peiser from Penland, North Carolina; photographed outside the resident barn at the Penland School of Craft, Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mark Peiser (born 1938) is a glass artist at the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Mark Peiser from Penland, North Carolina; photographed in front of the glass studio at the Penland School of Craft, Penland, North Carolina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composite photographic portraits with hand painting by Bea Nettles created 1970-2001, bulk 1970-1975, that depict the subjects as Major Arcana based on the Rider-Waite Tarot published by the Rider Company in 1909 based on the instructions of academic and mystic Arthur Edward Waite (1857-1942) and illustrated by Pamela Colman Smith (1878-1951).

The Minor Arcana are suit cards in a divination tarot pack. Each suit consists of four court cards (face cards) and ten pip cards numbered one (ace) to ten. The wands correspond to the clubs of modern playing cards.

The subjects of the portraits document artists at the Penland School of Craft in Penland, North Carolina, created primarily during residencies by Nettles, as well as portraits of friends created in Rochester, New York, and portraits of family members likely created in Gainesville, Florida, and Rochester, New York.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in ascending order of face value.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert Forsythe was a photographer who accompanied Evon Streetman (born 1932) to the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hand of Robert Forsythe from Tallahassee, Florida; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Lionel Suntop (born 1942) is a photographer and a founder of Light Impressions publishing as well as the spouse of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Lionel Suntop from Rochester, New York; photographed in Rochester, New York, likely near the shore of Lake Ontario

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Steve Kahn (1943-2018) was a photographer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Steve Kahn from San Francisco, California; photographed in Rochester, New York, likely on the shore of Lake Ontario

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: View of four tree branches arranged vertically in the ground with a three-story building in the background.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Eastham Clark (born 1952) is a medical doctor specializing in sports medicine married to Elizabeth Grace Nettle Clark, and a brother-in-law of Bea Nettles. Constance Louise Nettles Scott (born 1956) is a sister of Bea Nettles. John Robert Nettles (born 1948) is a lawyer and brother of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of John Eastham Clark with Constance Louise Nettles Scott and John Robert Nettles, all from Gainesville, Florida, with an unidentified man

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: William "Bill" Joseph Brown (born 1955) is owner of the Anvil Arts Studio and the eldest son of Jane Comfort Brennan Brown (born 1931), an arts administrator, and William "Bill" Joseph Brown (1923-1992), a designer and director of the Penland School of Crafts from 1962 to 1983.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of William Joseph Brown, Jr., from Penland, North Carolina; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John Eastham Clark (born 1952) is a medical doctor specializing in sports medicine married to Elizabeth Grace Nettle Clark, and a brother-in-law of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of John Eastham Clark from Gainesville, Florida

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composite photograph of eight exposures of a tree branch

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Peter Spider was a Core Fellow at the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Peter Spider from New Canaan, Connecticut; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robert Forsythe was a photographer who accompanied Evon Streetman (born 1932) to the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Robert Forsythe from Tallahassee, Florida; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of a subject identified as Joel, a student the Penland School of Craft; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jason Weidemier (born 1977) was a sculpture student at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Jason Weidemier from Champaign, Illinois; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Peter Desmond was a student at the Penland School of Craft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Peter Desmond from San Francisco, California; photographed at Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Marie Johanna Van Rennes Vrana (1926-2003) was the spouse of sculptor Albert Vrana (1921-1994).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Marie Johanna Van Rennes Vrana from Miami, Florida; photographed in a meadow at the summer home of Vrana in Penland, North Carolina

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Albert Sinclair Vrana (1921-1994) was a sculptor known for his monumental architectural sculptures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Albert Vrana from Miami, Florida; photographed in a meadow at the summer home of Vrana in Penland, North Carolina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Image used as a box label for publication of Mountain Dream Tarot , approximately 1975.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Constance Louise Nettles Scott (born 1956) is a sister of Bea Nettles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Constance Louise Nettles Scott from Gainesville, Florida; excised image used as box label for publication of Mountain Dream Tarot: A Deck of 78 Photographic Cards , approximately 1975; printed text on recto: Distributed by: Light Impressions Corp. / Box 3012 / Rochester, NY 14614
